methodology.repetitions: 3 per query (median) unless noted; Haskell uses 10-run median
methodology.query_ms: Time executing the Prolog query, excluding fact loading and initialization
methodology.total_ms: Wall-clock time from program start to exit
methodology.platform_sandbox: Linux 6.1.158 amd64 sandbox VM (SWI-Prolog, Rust WAM interpreter, native DFS)
methodology.platform_haskell: WSL2 4-core host (Haskell WAM + FFI, Rust WAM + FFI hand-tuned)

native_rust_dfs_pruned.notes: Native Rust DFS, skip paths longer than best-known distance

haskell_ffi_parallel.notes: From WAM_PERF_OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md, 10-run median, WSL2 host; parallelism over 386 seeds gives ~3.3x speedup

native_rust_dfs_unpruned.notes: Standard DFS with cycle detection and depth<=10; same algorithm as WAM without pruning

haskell_ffi_single_core.notes: From WAM_PERF_OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md, 10-run median, WSL2 host; FFI bypasses WAM interpreter for hot loop

rust_ffi_hand_tuned_phase_d.notes: From WAM_PERF_OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md; hand-inlined category_ancestor calls and fact lookups as native Rust. Uses FFI dispatch mechanism but kernels were written by hand, NOT automatic transpilation. Does not generalize.

rust_wam_interpreter.notes: generate_wam_effective_distance_benchmark.pl; full WAM instruction decode/dispatch; interpreter overhead > pruning benefit at this scale

swi_prolog_accumulated.notes: Reference SWI-Prolog implementation, optimized accumulated variant

swi_prolog_seeded.notes: Seeded variant, recomputes from each seed independently

go_wam.notes: Build OK after SharedWamCode fix; benchmark driver not yet wired up for fact loading

python_wam.notes: Runtime OK after module-qualified predicate fix; benchmark driver not yet connected

native_rust_unpruned.description: Standard DFS with cycle detection and depth<=10
native_rust_unpruned.notes: Measured in sandbox; establishes baseline for WAM interpreter overhead comparison

native_rust_pruned.description: DFS skipping paths longer than best-known distance
native_rust_pruned.notes: Measured in sandbox; represents optimized native baseline

analysis.wam_interpreter_vs_dfs: WAM interpreter (137ms) is slower than unpruned DFS (81ms) — interpreter overhead outweighs pruning benefit at scale 300
analysis.haskell_ffi_vs_dfs: Haskell FFI single-core (107ms query) competitive with native DFS because FFI bypasses WAM interpreter for hot category_parent/2 loop
analysis.haskell_parallel_advantage: 32ms (4-core) approaches pruned DFS (29ms) primarily from parallelism over 386 seeds (~3.3x speedup), not algorithmic improvement
analysis.unifyweaver_value: Haskell 107ms single-core was AUTOMATICALLY generated from Prolog source — no hand-written graph code. Rust 126ms FFI required manual kernel fusion that does not generalize.

bug_fixes: Go emitter: added SharedWamCode/SharedWamLabels exported aliases in wam_go_target.pl
Rust lowered emitter: added escape_rust_string for builtin_call ops in wam_rust_lowered_emitter.pl
Go lowered emitter: added escape_go_string for builtin_call ops in wam_go_lowered_emitter.pl
Python target: added Module:Pred/Arity handling in compile_one_predicate in wam_python_target.pl
